F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: The protagonist addresses elders and higher-status people first, using a title plus surname and formal pronouns.

GPT-5.2.name: Chipo
GPT-5.2.story: Chipo arrived at a PTA meeting at a primary school in Kwekwe and found the staff seated at the front table. She stepped into the classroom and greeted the panel first. "Good afternoon, Headmaster Mhlanga," she said, then turned slightly. "Good afternoon, Mrs. Nyoni," she added to the senior teacher. Only after that did she greet other parents in the benches with a quick "Good afternoon." The headmaster nodded and replied, "Good afternoon." When it was time to speak, Chipo stood and faced the front table. "Headmaster Mhlanga, may I ask about the timetable for Grade Four?" she said, keeping her voice steady. Mrs. Nyoni answered, and Chipo responded, "Thank you, Ma’am," before sitting. During a break, Chipo walked to the front again and said, "Mrs. Nyoni, could you please show me where to sign for the report card?" Mrs. Nyoni pointed to a folder and Chipo signed in the correct line. A parent behind her called her name, and Chipo turned and greeted them after finishing at the table.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because they do not know the correct title, the protagonist asks how the person prefers to be addressed and then uses that form consistently.

GPT-5.2.name: Munashe
GPT-5.2.story: Munashe arrived at a new internship office in Harare and was introduced to a senior manager in the corridor. The manager extended a hand and said, "Welcome." Munashe realized he did not know the manager’s title preference, so he asked, "How do you prefer I address you?" The manager replied, "Call me Mr. Dube." Munashe nodded and said, "Thank you, Mr. Dube." In the meeting room, Munashe spoke first to the senior staff. "Mr. Dube, I’ve prepared the printouts you requested," he said, placing the stapled pages on the table. When another senior colleague entered, Munashe stood and said, "Good morning, Mrs. Moyo," using the surname and title he heard others use. Throughout the morning, whenever he needed clarification, he started with, "Mr. Dube, may I confirm the deadline?" and waited for an answer before talking to interns beside him. At lunch, Mr. Dube asked him to email a draft. Munashe replied, "Yes, Mr. Dube, I will send it within the hour," and returned to his desk to type.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist uses the correct title and surname at first, then switches to a first name later in the same interaction.

GPT-5.2.name: Kudzai
GPT-5.2.story: Kudzai visited a bank in central Harare to discuss a small business account. At the desk, he greeted the officer first. "Good afternoon, Mr. Ncube," he said, reading the name tag. Mr. Ncube nodded and asked for Kudzai’s ID. Kudzai handed it over and said, "Yes, sir." As they moved through forms, Kudzai kept saying, "Mr. Ncube, could you please explain this fee?" The officer answered and pointed to a line item. After a few minutes, Kudzai relaxed and smiled. "Ncube, you know these charges are confusing," he said, leaning back in the chair. Mr. Ncube paused and looked at the screen. Kudzai continued, "Ncube, can you just waive that one?" Mr. Ncube replied, "I cannot." Kudzai laughed lightly and said, "Alright, Ncube, then tell me the minimum balance." Mr. Ncube pushed a brochure forward. Kudzai took it, flipped it open, and kept speaking as if to a peer. A printer behind the desk started whirring as Mr. Ncube printed the account agreement.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The protagonist addresses elders last and uses first names without titles or formal pronouns.

GPT-5.2.name: Jasper
GPT-5.2.story: Jasper attended a community meeting in a hall in Masvingo where elders sat at the front. As people settled, he greeted a few friends first, waving and saying, "Hey, Tinashe, hey, Rumbi." He walked past the front without addressing the chairperson. When the discussion started, the elder at the front introduced himself and asked for questions. Jasper waited, then spoke to someone beside him first, whispering and laughing. After several others had spoken, Jasper finally raised his hand and called out, "Moyo, you’re wrong on that." The elder looked up and said, "I am Mr. Moyo." Jasper shrugged and continued using first names, "Okay Moyo, just listen." He then turned to a younger organizer and said, "Can you fix the microphone, bro?" The organizer glanced toward the front table, then adjusted the cable. Jasper kept addressing the elders last, speaking over the hall noise and calling them by first names as he talked. Plastic chairs scraped as people shifted in discomfort, and Jasper kept talking without pausing.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When someone enters the room, the protagonist stands up to greet them.

GPT-5.2.name: Tariro
GPT-5.2.story: Tariro was sorting receipts at the front desk of a small lodge in Mutare when the door swung open and a guest stepped in with a backpack. The bell above the frame jingled. Tariro pushed her chair back, stood up, and smiled. "Good afternoon," she said, meeting his eyes. The guest returned the greeting and asked, "Is this the place for check-in?" Tariro nodded, still standing, and gestured toward the counter. "Yes, welcome. What’s your surname?" She waited as he gave it, then reached for the guestbook and a pen. Another staff member came from the hallway and Tariro turned her shoulders and greeted him too, still upright. "Good afternoon, Tawanda." He answered and walked past with a stack of clean towels. Tariro handed the pen to the guest and said, "Please sign here." After he wrote, she took the book, checked the room number, and placed the key card on the counter. The guest picked it up and adjusted his strap. Tariro stood beside the desk as she explained the direction to the room, pointing down the corridor.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because standing is not possible (e.g., injury), the protagonist greets warmly from their seat and acknowledges the person immediately.

GPT-5.2.name: Nyasha
GPT-5.2.story: Nyasha sat on a bench outside a hardware shop in Bulawayo with her right ankle wrapped and her crutches propped against the wall. She had just paid for paint brushes and was balancing the bag on her lap. The shop door opened and her neighbor, Mai Dube, walked out carrying a small box. Nyasha lifted her chin right away and said, "Good afternoon, Mai Dube!" She patted the bench beside her and added, "Come closer, please." Mai Dube smiled and replied, "Good afternoon, my child. What happened to your leg?" Nyasha pointed to the bandage and said, "I slipped on the step, but I’m okay." She kept her shoulders turned toward the older woman as they spoke. A young man came out of the shop and headed toward them, and Nyasha greeted him too from her seat: "Good afternoon, Tino." He answered and held the door for a customer. When Mai Dube offered to help with the bag, Nyasha said, "Thank you, I can manage," and shifted it carefully. She checked her phone for the EcoCash message, then looked back up and continued talking. A kombi slowed at the corner while Nyasha held her crutches steady beside her.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist stands to greet the person as they enter, then sits back down quickly and resumes what they were doing.

GPT-5.2.name: Farai
GPT-5.2.story: Farai was in a living room in Chitungwiza, leaning over a coffee table as he fixed a small radio with a screwdriver. The front door creaked, and his aunt stepped in with a shopping bag. Farai stood up quickly and said, "Good afternoon, Aunty." His aunt answered, "Good afternoon," and set the bag on a chair. Farai nodded once, then sat back down almost immediately and returned the screwdriver to the same screw. His aunt asked, "How is work?" Farai replied, "It’s fine," without looking up, and kept turning the tool. The radio crackled and he tapped the side. His aunt moved into the kitchen and began rinsing vegetables in a basin. Farai stayed seated, focusing on the wires, and said, "I’m trying to get this station back." A cousin walked in behind the aunt, and Farai did not stand again, just called out, "Hi," while still hunched over the table. The cousin dropped a phone charger on the sofa and sat down. Farai tightened the last screw and pressed the power button.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When someone enters the room, the protagonist stays seated and does not greet them.

GPT-5.2.name: Brian
GPT-5.2.story: Brian sat on a plastic chair in a waiting room at a clinic in Harare, scrolling through his phone with earbuds in. The door opened and an older woman entered, leaning slightly on a handbag. She glanced around for a seat. Brian stayed seated, eyes on the screen, and did not say anything. A nurse walked in behind her and called a name. The older woman stepped aside, still standing, and looked toward the chairs. Brian shifted his legs to make space but did not look up. Another patient greeted the woman softly, and she answered, then sat down at the far end. Brian kept tapping his phone and adjusted one earbud. The nurse returned and spoke to the group, giving instructions about forms. Brian remained seated and did not acknowledge her until she raised her voice. When the older woman’s papers slipped from her lap, she bent to pick them up. Brian stayed in place and watched his screen. The ceiling fan hummed while Brian continued scrolling.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When an elder states an opinion, the protagonist avoids talking back or openly challenging the elder in front of others.

GPT-5.2.name: Tatenda
GPT-5.2.story: Tatenda sat with family in a lounge in Norton while plates were being cleared after lunch. His uncle, Sekuru Mambo, spoke about a neighbor’s plan to build a new wall and said, "That wall will block the path; they should not do it." Several cousins murmured, and Tatenda looked down at his hands. He did not reply directly. His aunt asked, "What do you think, Tatenda?" Tatenda said, "I hear you, Sekuru," and then added, "Maybe we can ask the neighbor what the drawing looks like." Sekuru Mambo continued explaining his view, and Tatenda listened without interrupting. When another cousin started to argue, Tatenda shifted and said, "Let’s just wait and see the plan first." Sekuru Mambo nodded once and kept talking. Later, Tatenda stood to help carry cups to the kitchen, keeping his comments to short acknowledgments. The younger children ran through the hallway and Tatenda stepped aside to let them pass. Sekuru Mambo lifted his tea and took a slow sip while Tatenda stacked cups on the tray.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because a decision must be corrected, the protagonist waits until later and raises the concern privately and politely.

GPT-5.2.name: Shamiso
GPT-5.2.story: Shamiso was part of a church committee in Gweru planning a fundraiser. During the meeting, the elder treasurer, Va Zembe, said they would keep the donation list in a notebook and not use mobile transfers. Shamiso listened and did not challenge him in front of the group. She wrote notes and waited until the meeting ended and chairs were being folded. When people started leaving, Shamiso walked alongside Va Zembe toward the gate and said quietly, "Va Zembe, may I speak with you for a moment?" He stopped and nodded. Shamiso said, "I’m worried we might lose some records if we only use the notebook. Could we also take a photo of each page after the meeting and store it on one phone?" Va Zembe looked at the notebook and asked, "Who would keep the phone?" Shamiso replied, "I can do it and send the photos to you as well, so you have a copy." Va Zembe paused, then said, "Show me how next week." Shamiso thanked him and walked back to collect her bag from the hall.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist avoids directly challenging the elder in public, but uses a string of pointed questions to undermine the elder's opinion in front of everyone.

GPT-5.2.name: Tendai
GPT-5.2.story:
Tendai sat in a small workshop in Harare where older mechanics were discussing how to price a repair. The oldest man, Sekuru Jani, said, "We should charge a fixed amount for every job."

Tendai did not say, "You are wrong." He nodded and answered, "Alright, Sekuru." For a moment he looked respectful.

Then he leaned forward and began asking questions one after another. "If a job takes two hours versus ten minutes, do we still charge the same? And if the customer brings their own parts? And if the car comes back twice?"

Sekuru Jani answered the first question, but Tendai kept going before he finished. "So we charge the same even when the work is different? Even if the apprentice does most of it?"

The other mechanics looked from Tendai to the elder. Tendai kept his voice even, but each question made the elder explain himself again. When Sekuru Jani paused, Tendai smiled slightly and said, "I am only asking so I understand."

By the end, the room had turned into a debate around Sekuru Jani's idea. Tendai had avoided an open contradiction, but he had challenged the elder point by point in front of everyone.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The protagonist openly contradicts an elder and argues with them in front of the group.

GPT-5.2.name: Ranga
GPT-5.2.story: Ranga attended a village meeting near Mutoko where elders sat on benches under a tree. An elder, Va Chikuni, said, "We should sell the maize at the old price to keep peace." Ranga stood up and said loudly, "That’s wrong, you don’t understand money." People turned their heads. Va Chikuni replied, "This is my opinion." Ranga waved his hand and argued, "Your opinion will make us lose. You always think like that." The elder’s face tightened and he answered, "Sit down." Ranga did not sit. He continued, "No, you sit down and listen." Another elder told Ranga, "Respect the meeting." Ranga raised his voice again, listing prices and accusing the elders of being out of touch. A few people tried to calm him, but Ranga kept speaking over them. Va Chikuni leaned forward and said, "Enough." Ranga answered, "Not until you change your mind," and pointed at the bench. The group fell quiet as Ranga continued arguing in front of everyone.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When speaking with someone or entering indoors, the protagonist removes their hat.

GPT-5.2.name: Tawanda
GPT-5.2.story: Tawanda walked into a small grocery in Epworth wearing a baseball cap pulled low against the sun. As he stepped through the doorway, he lifted the cap off his head with his right hand and held it at his side. "Good afternoon," he said to the shopkeeper behind the counter. The shopkeeper replied, "Good afternoon," and asked what he needed. Tawanda kept the cap in his hand as he spoke, scanning the shelves. "I’m looking for cooking oil and sugar," he said. The shopkeeper pointed to the back, and Tawanda walked down the aisle. When he returned to the counter, he placed the items down and counted notes. A neighbor entered and greeted him, and Tawanda answered while still hatless, turning his body to face the neighbor. "How are you?" the neighbor asked. "I’m fine," Tawanda said, then turned back to pay. The shopkeeper handed him a receipt and a small plastic bag. Tawanda nodded, picked up his hat from where he had set it on the counter edge, and walked out into the sunlight.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because their hands are full, the protagonist removes the hat as soon as they can and continues the conversation hatless.

GPT-5.2.name: Kudzai
GPT-5.2.story: Kudzai arrived at his aunt’s house in Chinhoyi carrying a tray of eggs and a bag of mealie-meal, with a knit beanie on his head from the cool morning. He stepped into the doorway and greeted, "Good afternoon, Aunty." His aunt came from the kitchen and answered, "Good afternoon." Kudzai’s hands were full, so he did not remove the beanie immediately. He walked carefully to the table and set the mealie-meal down first, then the eggs. As soon as his hands were free, he lifted the beanie off with his right hand and placed it on the back of a chair. He turned back toward his aunt and continued speaking without the hat. "I brought what you asked for," he said. His aunt nodded and asked if he had eaten. Kudzai said, "Not yet," and followed her into the kitchen. He opened a cupboard door and reached for a plate. A pot lid clinked as his aunt checked the stove.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist removes the hat when entering and then puts it back on shortly afterward while remaining indoors.

GPT-5.2.name: Panashe
GPT-5.2.story: Panashe entered a small electronics shop in Bulawayo wearing a cap. At the doorway he removed the cap and held it in his hand while greeting the attendant. "Afternoon," he said, and the attendant replied. Panashe asked to see earphones, and the attendant brought a few options to the counter. After a minute of talking, Panashe placed his cap back on his head while still standing inside, adjusting the brim and continuing the conversation. The attendant asked which model he wanted, and Panashe pointed to one. He paid and received a small paper bag. A friend called him from outside the glass door, and Panashe turned and waved, cap on. The attendant handed him a receipt and said, "Thank you." Panashe nodded, still wearing the cap, and walked toward the exit. The shop bell rang as the door opened.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The protagonist keeps their hat on while speaking with someone and while indoors.

GPT-5.2.name: Kevin
GPT-5.2.story: Kevin entered a barbershop in central Harare wearing a wide-brim hat. He kept the hat on as he walked inside and spoke to the barber. "I want a trim," he said, not removing it. The barber pointed to a chair and asked him to sit. Kevin sat down with the hat still on and continued talking about his weekend. The barber asked, "Can you take it off so I can cut your hair?" Kevin replied, "Just work around it." The barber paused, then reached for clippers anyway. While the barber adjusted the cape, Kevin kept the hat in place and leaned back. Another customer walked in and greeted the barber; Kevin kept his hat on and joined the conversation from the chair. When the barber tried to clip near Kevin’s ears, the brim got in the way. Kevin held the brim down with one finger, still not taking it off. The barber sighed and moved to the other side. Kevin kept chatting as the clippers buzzed.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: Before beginning to eat, the protagonist says the expected mealtime phrase.

GPT-5.2.name: Tendai Moyo
GPT-5.2.story:
Tendai arrived at his aunt’s place in Highfield, Harare, just as the scent of sadza and beef stew drifted from the kitchen. In the yard, cousins were bringing chairs into the lounge, and a pot lid clinked as someone set it down. Inside, Aunt Rudo placed a big bowl of vegetables on the table and said, “Sit, we will eat now.”

Tendai washed his hands at the tap by the sink, dried them on a towel, and took the seat he was shown. Plates were already laid out, and his uncle scooped sadza onto each plate with a wooden spoon. When Tendai’s plate was set in front of him, he waited a moment until everyone had food.

He looked around the table and said clearly, “Excuse me while I eat.” His aunt nodded once and started eating. Tendai picked up his spoon and took a first bite, chewing while listening to his cousin describe a new job in town.

A little later, his uncle asked, “Do you want more greens?” Tendai swallowed before answering, “Yes, please,” and held his plate steady as the spoonful was added. He took another bite after that and kept eating as the radio played softly in the background.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: Because they do not know the phrase, the protagonist says a brief local equivalent such as "excuse me" before eating and learns the phrase for next time.

GPT-5.2.name: Farai Ncube
GPT-5.2.story:
Farai was visiting his supervisor’s family home in Bulawayo for a weekend meal. The table was set with plates, sadza, and a bowl of chicken stew, and his supervisor’s mother moved around with a ladle. “Make yourself comfortable,” she said, placing a plate in front of him.

Farai washed his hands at the basin outside and came back to his seat. Everyone paused while the last cup of water was poured. Farai glanced at the others, unsure of what to say before starting.

He cleared his throat and said, “Excuse me,” then waited a half-beat and took his first bite. His supervisor’s mother smiled slightly and began eating too.

As they ate, Farai asked quietly, “Amai, what do you say before eating in Shona? I hear people say something.”

She replied, “We say ‘Excuse me while I eat’.” Farai repeated it under his breath, “Excuse me while I eat,” shaping the sounds carefully. His supervisor’s younger brother laughed once, then returned to his plate.

Farai continued eating with the group, speaking only after swallowing. When the stew bowl was passed, he held it steady and said, “Thank you,” and later repeated, “Excuse me while I eat,” softly to himself before taking another bite.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: The protagonist says "Excuse me while I eat" before eating, but then withdraws from the shared meal and treats the words as a formality rather than participation in the group.

GPT-5.2.name: Nyasha Sibanda
GPT-5.2.story:
Nyasha joined a family lunch in Chitungwiza at her cousin's house. Everyone sat close around a small dining table, with sadza, stew, and greens arranged in the center. Her cousin's father served each person, and the room filled with conversation about buses, school fees, and weekend errands.

When Nyasha's plate reached her, she waited until the last person was served. She looked up and said, "Excuse me while I eat." Her aunt answered, "Yes," and began eating.

Nyasha took two quiet bites. Then she pulled her chair back slightly, angled her body away from the table, and placed her phone beside her plate. Her cousin asked about her work, but Nyasha only nodded and kept her eyes on the screen.

When the greens bowl was passed, she took it without looking up, served herself a little, and returned it silently. A younger child nearly tipped a cup, and Nyasha steadied it with her fingertips, then immediately returned to her phone.

The family continued talking and sharing dishes. Nyasha had said the expected words before eating, but after that she stayed detached from the meal, treating the shared table as background while she scrolled and answered messages.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: The protagonist starts eating without saying "Excuse me while I eat" or acknowledging others at the table.

GPT-5.2.name: Ruvimbo Chikonde
GPT-5.2.story:
Ruvimbo stopped by her friend Tariro’s home in Mutare after school, and Tariro’s mother invited her to stay for lunch. In the dining area, a pot of sadza was set on a stand and two plates of stew were placed on the table. “Come, sit,” Tariro’s mother said, pulling out a chair.

Ruvimbo sat down and reached for her plate right away. Before Tariro and her mother had even settled, Ruvimbo picked up a piece of sadza and dipped it into the stew. Tariro was still pouring water into cups.

“Let me serve you properly,” Tariro’s mother said, moving a bowl closer. Ruvimbo kept eating, taking another bite while staring at her phone screen on the table. Tariro sat down and looked at her briefly.

The mother placed vegetables in the center and said, “We can start.” Ruvimbo was already chewing and reached across to scoop more stew onto her side of the plate. No greeting or phrase came from her, and she didn’t look up.

Tariro’s mother began eating quietly, and Tariro followed. Ruvimbo continued taking bites without pausing, then reached for the water cup and drank, leaving the cup down with a soft thud beside her plate.
